mission.title: Install and Configure Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK)
mission.description: Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK) automates the deployment and management of the Elastic Stack on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users looking to run Elasticsearch, Kibana, and other Elastic products in a Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include the complexity of managing Kubernetes resources and the need for familiarity with the Elastic Stack.

steps.title: Add the ECK Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add elastic https://Helm.elastic.co

helm repo update

```

> This is the official Helm repository for ECK.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for ECK to manage Elasticsearch and Kibana resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://download.elastic.co/downloads/eck/2.14.0/crds.yaml

```

> This installs the required CRDs for ECK.

steps.title: Install the ECK operator
steps.description:
This step installs the ECK operator in the 'elastic-system' namespace.

```bash

helm install eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --create-namespace --version 2.14.0

```

> Installs the ECK operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the ECK operator installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the ECK operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n elastic-system

```

> You should see the ECK operator pod running.

steps.title: Create an Elasticsearch cluster
steps.description:
This step creates an Elasticsearch cluster using a custom resource definition.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: default
spec:
  version: 8.0.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

```

> This creates a 3-node Elasticsearch cluster.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: default
spec:
  version: 8.0.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Elasticsearch cluster
steps.description:
This command checks if the Elasticsearch cluster is running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -n default

```

> You should see your Elasticsearch cluster with a status of 'green'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the ECK operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ECK operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all Elasticsearch clusters managed by ECK, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall eck-operator -n elastic-system

kubectl delete namespace elastic-system

```

> Uninstalls the ECK operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the ECK operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Elasticsearch data. This step will upgrade the ECK operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --version 2.14.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n elastic-system

```

> Drains the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades the ECK operator to the specified version.
> Uncordons the node after the upgrade.
> Verifies that the ECK operator pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRDs'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the CRDs are already installed or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the existing CRDs with 'kubectl get crd' and ensure your network is stable.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Elasticsearch cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Elasticsearch nodes are not starting correctly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and ensure sufficient resources are allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm install failed: chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified Helm chart version does not exist in the repository.

**Fix:**
Verify the chart version with 'helm search repo elastic/eck-operator' and update the version accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply failed: no matches for kind 'Elasticsearch' in version 'elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This suggests that the CRDs have not been installed or are outdated.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://download.elastic.co/downloads/eck/2.14.0/crds.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Elasticsearch 8.0, improved TLS certificate management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.13, ensure to update your Elasticsearch custom resources to be compatible with version 8.0.

resolution.summary: You should see the Elasticsearch cluster with a status of 'green' when running 'kubectl get elasticsearch -n default'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: default
spec:
  version: 8.0.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
